---
title: "Profiles overview"
description: "Builder, account_assistant, personal_assistant — AWS blast radius per profile."
---

Every Lowkey install picks exactly one **profile**. The profile determines:

1. **IAM policy** attached to the EC2 instance profile
2. **Instance size** in simple mode
3. **Which security services** auto-enable
4. **Which packs are compatible** (a few packs are profile-gated)

## Quick comparison

| | **builder** | **account_assistant** | **personal_assistant** |
|---|---|---|---|
| IAM | AdministratorAccess | ReadOnlyAccess + targeted writes | Bedrock only |
| Instance (simple) | `t4g.xlarge` | `t4g.medium` | `t4g.medium` |
| Security Hub | ✅ on | ✅ on | ❌ off |
| GuardDuty | ✅ on | ✅ on | ❌ off |
| Inspector | ✅ on | ✅ on | ❌ off |
| Access Analyzer | ✅ on | ✅ on | ❌ off |
| Config recorder | ✅ on | ✅ on | ❌ off |
| Typical use | Agent writes code + deploys AWS resources | Agent explains / audits existing AWS | Agent runs Bedrock inference only |

## Picking a profile

<CardGroup cols={3}>
  <Card title="Builder" icon="hammer" href="/profiles/builder">
    Full AWS admin. Agent can create VPCs, Lambdas, CloudFront, whatever. **Dedicated sandbox only.**
  </Card>
  <Card title="Account assistant" icon="magnifying-glass" href="/profiles/account-assistant">
    Read-only plus a narrow set of writes. Safe for dev or staging accounts.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Personal assistant" icon="user" href="/profiles/personal-assistant">
    Bedrock inference only — no AWS API surface. Use for coding help without giving the agent your AWS keys.
  </Card>
</CardGroup>

## Compatibility with packs

Most packs work with all three profiles. Exceptions:

- **NemoClaw** requires `personal_assistant` — it runs in an OpenShell sandbox that blocks all AWS API calls.
- Other packs take a warning/note in builder mode but work fine with any profile.

If you pick an incompatible combination, the installer refuses the install and suggests alternatives.

## Overriding defaults

Advanced mode lets you pick any instance type, toggle any security service, supply your own VPC/subnet, etc. See [install.sh CLI reference](/reference/cli) for the full flag list.
